sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.35 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: Suppose that$H$is a finite linear group over${ C}$and$h$is an element of$H$of prime order$p$which is not contained in any abelian normal subgroup. Is it true that$h$has at least$(p - 1)/2$different eigenvalues? { A.\,E.\,Zalesskii} Yes, it is (G.\,R.\,Robinson, J.~Algebra, { 178}, no.\,2 (1995), 635--642).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
